channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇨🇳 China warns US-Philippines-Japan drills risk regional trust
item.description: China criticized expanded military cooperation as US and Philippines began annual exercises with Japan participation. The drills include maritime strike operations near Taiwan.

item.title: 🇵🇭 US-Philippines military exercises test real-world readiness
item.description: Philippine and US forces launched annual exercises with maritime strike drills near Taiwan. Manila military chief said operations would test readiness under real-world conditions.

item.title: 📉 Gulf stocks fall on renewed US-Iran tensions
item.description: Gulf equities declined on fears the US-Iran ceasefire could unravel after the US seized an Iranian cargo vessel. Traffic through the Strait of Hormuz remained largely suspended.

item.title: 🥇 Gold eases as investors await clarity on US-Iran talks
item.description: Gold prices fell as the dollar strengthened, with markets watching whether US-Iran peace talks will proceed this week after renewed tensions over the weekend.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Britain Sidelined in Carney-Backed Defense Bank Talks
item.description: The UK Treasury has expressed little enthusiasm for a Mark Carney-backed initiative to provide lower-cost loans to finance armed forces, leaving Britain on the margins as allies push for defense infrastructure investment.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Commission Jobs Reshuffle on Hold as Chiefs Stay Put
item.description: The first serious round of movement in the upper ranks since Ursula von der Leyen took office has stalled, with key commissioners remaining in their posts longer than anticipated.

item.title: 🇲🇽 Canadian Tourist Killed in Shooting at Mexican Pyramid Site
item.description: A Canadian woman was killed and several others injured in a shooting at the Teotihuacan pyramids, a major tourist destination near Mexico City. The attacker later died by suicide.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey Jails Eight Over Deadly 2024 Cable Car Accident
item.description: A Turkish court sentenced eight people to prison terms for a 2024 cable car accident that killed one person and injured multiple others at a popular tourist destination in southern Turkey.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israels Independence Day Marked Amid War Clouds
item.description: Cities across Israel moved ahead with Independence Day celebrations despite the fragile ceasefires with Lebanon and Iran, though some municipalities opted for scaled-down community-based events.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Scraps Ban on Lethal Weapons Exports in Historic Policy Shift
item.description: Japan has approved ending its ban on lethal weapons exports, marking the biggest change to its postwar pacifist policy as the country seeks to build up its arms industry and deepen defense cooperation with partners.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Labor Secretary Chavez-DeRemer Resigns Amid Misconduct Investigation
item.description: Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer has resigned from President Trump's cabinet after allegations of abuse of power, including an affair with a subordinate and drinking on the job. She is the third woman to leave the administration since March.

item.title: 🇨🇺 Cuba Confirms Talks With US, Seeks End to Energy Blockade
item.description: A Cuban Foreign Ministry official confirmed a 'respectful and professional' exchange with US officials, as Cuba seeks an end to Trump's energy blockade amid ongoing tensions.

item.title: 🌍 UN Says US Iran War Spending Could Have Saved 87 Million Lives
item.description: The head of the UN humanitarian agency expressed frustration that the $2 billion weekly cost of the Iran conflict comes amid major cuts to aid budgets, noting the funds could have saved millions globally.

item.title: 🇵🇸 Gaza Needs $71 Billion to Rebuild After Devastating War, Study Shows
item.description: A new study by the EU, UN and World Bank estimates Gaza needs $71.4 billion over the next decade to recover from two years of war that devastated most of the Palestinian territory.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia Launches Deadliest Aerial Assault of 2026 on Ukraine
item.description: Russia attacked Ukraine with nearly 700 drones and 19 ballistic missiles, killing 16 people including a 12-year-old child. The assault struck Kyiv, Odesa, Dnipro, and energy infrastructure in the southeast.

item.title: 🇮🇷 US Seizes Iranian Cargo Ship in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US Marines seized an Iranian-flagged vessel in the Strait of Hormuz after the destroyer USS Spruance disabled its propulsion. Iran warned of retaliation, saying it will not participate in peace talks under threats.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Prices Jump Over 4% After Iranian Ship Seizure
item.description: Brent crude rose to $94.20 per barrel after Trump announced the seizure of an Iranian cargo ship. Markets remain volatile as Iran re-closed the Strait of Hormuz and threatened to target approaching ships.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Largest US-Philippines Military Exercise Begins as China Warns Against Playing With Fire
item.description: Over 17,000 troops from US, Australia, Philippines, Japan and other nations began the Balikatan exercises. Japan sent 1,400 troops for the first time, while China warned participating countries they are escalating tensions.

item.title: 🇧🇬🇪🇺 Pro-Russian ex-president Radev wins Bulgaria snap elections
item.description: Rumen Radevs Progressive Bulgaria won 44.6% of the vote, positioning the Kremlin-friendly party to govern alone and marking a victory for pro-Russian representation in the EU.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇺🇸 Zelensky criticizes US envoys for visiting Moscow but not Kyiv
item.description: President Zelensky called it disrespectful that US envoys Witkoff and Kushner have flown to Moscow multiple times but have not yet visited Kyiv, as peace talks with Russia remain stalled.

item.title: 🇺🇦🛡️ Ukraine aims to develop domestic ballistic defense system within a year
item.description: President Zelensky announced an ambitious timeline to develop a European anti-ballistic missile defense system with European partners, seeking to reduce reliance on US Patriot systems.

item.title: ⛪🏛️ Pope Leo XIV joins tradition of pontiffs sparring with world leaders
item.description: The popes veiled rebukes of Trump over the Iran war place him in a millennia-spanning tradition of pontiffs clashing with political figures from Attila the Hun to Napoleon.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Overhauls Defense Export Rules, Opens Door to Global Arms Market
item.description: Japan scrapped decades-old restrictions on weapons exports, now allowing warships, missiles and fighter jets to be sold abroad. The major policy shift marks a departure from Japan's postwar pacifist constitution amid rising regional tensions.

item.title: 🇳🇿 New Zealand PM Luxon Survives Leadership Confidence Vote
item.description: Prime Minister Christopher Luxon retained the backing of his National Party lawmakers after calling a confidence vote amid sliding poll numbers months before the general election. He left without taking questions from reporters.

item.title: 🛢️ Gulf States Fear Iran Could Cement Control of Hormuz Strait
item.description: Gulf nations worry that US-Iran peace talks may only secure limited outcomes, potentially leaving Tehran with lasting leverage over the strategic Strait of Hormuz. Former Russian President Medvedev's warning crystallised regional fears.
